metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the parent plan's child decomposition to move 97f2, 625b, 21ca, aff0, ad44, and eefd into the 'Landed and closed' section with their respective Gerrit change numbers.
evidence: Parent plan: 'Open (the remaining neutrality work, disjoint edit-sets, independently reviewable, parallel-safe): 97f2-2d98-2eff-41c1 … Canonical-shape outbound field diff … Canonical link diff … Vendor absence probe … Canonicalize reconcile_check.py … Rich-text codec …'
child 97f2 footer: 'Implemented and merged to main as Gerrit change 926 (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1).'
child 625b footer: 'Implemented and merged to main as Gerrit change 931'
child 21ca footer: 'Implemented and merged to main as Gerrit change 936'
child aff0 footer: 'Implemented and merged to main as Gerrit change 927'
child ad44 footer: 'Implemented and merged to main as Gerrit change 945'
child eefd footer: 'Implemented and merged to main as Gerrit change 935'
findings.finding: The parent plan's child-decomposition section labels six children (97f2, 625b, 21ca, aff0, ad44, eefd) as 'Open (the remaining neutrality work)' but every one of those children carries a 'Landed' footer with a concrete Gerrit change number (926, 931, 936, 927, 945, 935). Any reader relying on the parent plan as a status tracker will incorrectly treat already-merged work as outstanding, and the AC4/AC5 verification statements ('green on main', 'added by the open children') become misleading once the plan's status section is stale.
findings.location: Parent plan — 'Open (the remaining neutrality work)' paragraph; children 97f2, 625b, 21ca, aff0, ad44, eefd — 'Landed' footers
scenarios: A project manager reading the parent plan to triage remaining work incorrectly schedules all six stories as not-yet-started.
AC4 says the sweep gate is 'added by the open children … green on main' — with all children landed, the gate is already present; the 'open' framing makes it sound like future work.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the six children from the 'Open' paragraph to the 'Landed and closed' list alongside S1–S5 and 4af8, appending their change numbers (926, 931, 936, 927, 945, 935) in order.

findings.finding: aff0's approach description references stale run_differs.py line numbers (:230, :234, :369) that reflect the pre-4af8 state of the file. 4af8 is already landed and has modified run_differs.py to inject the backend object at the differ call sites (:481-495). The hoist logic ('today the backend is loaded at run_differs.py:234 — AFTER the probe dispatch call at :230') describes a file state that no longer exists, leaving the implementer without accurate coordinates for the required edit.
findings.location: child aff0-b0d9-e11c-4b6a — Approach section, run_differs.py paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Update aff0's Approach section to say 'after 4af8 lands, re-grep run_differs.py for the current backend-load site and probe-dispatch call; hoist the backend resolution if and only if the probe dispatch still precedes the backend assignment in the post-4af8 function body; update line-number references accordingly.'

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: etc.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Amend the parent success criterion to reflect that outbound_differ.py and run_differs.py are shared edit targets, explicitly listing them in the 'shared _backend.py/adapters/jira/backend.py' additive-edits carve-out (or replace 'disjoint core modules' with 'non-overlapping logical concerns').
evidence: Parent SC: 'Every deliverable above is covered by exactly one child; children do not overlap (each owns disjoint core modules; shared _backend.py/adapters/jira/backend.py edits are additive per child and recorded in each child's file impact).'

child 4af8: 'outbound_differ.compute_outbound_mutations: injected outbound_mapper … module-level _map_local_to_jira_fields import dropped'; 'run_differs: production injection point — obtains the configured Backend once and threads backend.outbound / backend.inbound into both differ phases'
child 625b Scope: 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py'; 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py'
child eefd G1G2 remediation: 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py is ADDED to Scope — it passes the SupportsLinks capability as the new 4th argument to _diff_links'
child aff0 Scope: 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py (capability-guarded routing at the probe call site)'
findings.finding: The parent success criterion explicitly states 'children do not overlap (each owns disjoint core modules)' but outbound_differ.py is in scope for three separate children (4af8 injects the mapper, 625b canonicalises the comparison and deletes the vendor helpers, eefd threads the SupportsLinks capability in), and run_differs.py is in scope for four (4af8, 625b, aff0, eefd). This is a direct SC self-contradiction.
findings.location: Parent plan — Success Criteria paragraph; child 4af8 Scope; child 625b Scope; child eefd Scope (G1G2 remediation gap 1); child aff0 Scope
scenarios: An implementor following the parent plan's 'disjoint' guarantee attempts to assign outbound_differ.py to a single owner and finds three separate sets of edits targeting it, leading to merge conflicts or missed co-ordination.
A completion verifier checking 'exactly one child per deliverable' cannot satisfy the criterion for outbound_differ.py or run_differs.py because both are multi-child.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the SC sentence to: 'children do not overlap on logical concerns (outbound_differ.py and run_differs.py are shared edit targets across 4af8/625b/eefd/aff0 with additive, sequenced edits; _backend.py and adapters/jira/backend.py are additive per child as already noted).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign ticket IDs to the four un-IDed open children ('Canonical-shape outbound field diff', 'Canonical link diff', 'Vendor absence probe behind SupportsAbsenceProbe', 'Rich-text codec + comment limits behind the port') so the Success Criteria bijection check and AC bullet 4 attribution are concretely verifiable.
evidence: 'Canonical-shape outbound field diff', 'Canonical link diff', 'Vendor absence probe behind SupportsAbsenceProbe', and 'Rich-text codec + comment limits behind the port' appear with no ticket ID
By contrast, 97f2 and ad44 carry explicit IDs in the same list
Success Criteria states: 'every deliverable above is covered by exactly one child; children do not overlap'
Without IDs the bijection check in AC bullet 4 ('added by the open children') cannot be attributed to a specific trackable unit
findings.finding: Four of the six open children in the Child decomposition section have no ticket ID, yet the Success Criteria requires 'every deliverable above is covered by exactly one child.' An executor establishing tracking links, assigning ownership, or verifying bijective coverage cannot satisfy that criterion as stated for the unnamed children.
findings.location: Child decomposition — Open children list; Success Criteria paragraph
scenarios: An executor assigning the canonical-diff child cannot create a 'depends_on' link without an ID
A reviewer verifying AC bullet 4 ('neutrality tests … added by the open children') cannot confirm each test file maps to exactly one tracked child
If two engineers independently start 'canonical link diff' and 'outbound field diff' work, there is no ticket to check for overlap
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short-form ID (same UUID-fragment style as the other children, e.g. `XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX`) to each bullet's heading line in the Open children list, mirroring the pattern of `97f2-2d98-2eff-41c1` and `ad44-9f46-07aa-480c`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC bullet (or extend the canonical-comparison AC) that explicitly verifies: (a) `ProbeBranch`/`ProbeResult` are defined in a core module, and (b) `adapters/jira/probe.py` exists and `SupportsAbsenceProbe` is implemented there.
evidence: 'Jira REST probe mechanics move to adapters/jira/probe.py; neutral ProbeBranch/ProbeResult vocabulary stays in core' (decomposition)
The zero-vendor-reach-through AC verifies only the absence of vendor imports, not the presence of ProbeBranch/ProbeResult in core or the existence of probe.py in adapters/jira/
findings.finding: The absence-probe open child describes two positive deliverables — `ProbeBranch`/`ProbeResult` neutral vocabulary introduced into core, and probe mechanics relocated to `adapters/jira/probe.py` — but no AC bullet verifies either the creation of those core types or the existence of the new adapter module; only the removal side (no vendor imports in core) is covered by the zero-vendor-reach-through AC.
findings.location: Child decomposition — 'Vendor absence probe behind SupportsAbsenceProbe' bullet; Acceptance Criteria — zero-vendor-reach-through bullet
scenarios: An implementation that deletes the probe entirely (satisfying 'no vendor import') would pass the AC without delivering the neutral vocabulary or the adapter module — the replacement half of the migration is unverified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC clause for `ad44` that verifies the positive side: `reconcile_check.py`'s local-vs-snapshot comparison routes through `InboundMapper` canonicalization and `assignee_identity` membership, confirmed by dedicated characterization or unit tests green on main.
evidence: 'the checker's local-vs-snapshot comparison reuses the canonical differ machinery (InboundMapper canonicalization, assignee_identity membership, sanitizer description parity)' (decomposition)

The zero-vendor-reach-through AC mentions only 'removal of that gate's documented reconcile_check carve-out' — a structural/import gate, not behavioral verification of the three canonicalization properties
findings.finding: The `ad44-9f46-07aa-480c` open child describes three positive behaviors — reuse of `InboundMapper` canonicalization, `assignee_identity` membership check, and sanitizer description parity — but the only AC coverage is the removal of the reconcile_check carve-out from the neutrality-test gate; none of the positive behavioral properties are verified by any AC bullet.
findings.location: Child decomposition — 'Canonicalize reconcile_check.py (ad44-9f46-07aa-480c)' bullet; Acceptance Criteria — zero-vendor-reach-through bullet
scenarios: An implementation that removes the carve-out and deletes the relevant comparison logic entirely would satisfy the AC; the positive behavioral contract (correct canonical comparison) would go unverified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign ticket IDs to the four anonymous open children (canonical outbound field diff, canonical link diff, vendor absence probe, rich-text codec + comment limits) so that the success-criteria 'exactly one child' guarantee is traceable.
evidence: 'Every deliverable above is covered by exactly one child' (Success Criteria)
Only 97f2 and ad44 carry ticket IDs among the open children; the four remaining open children are anonymous bullets
findings.finding: Four of the six open children (canonical-shape outbound field diff, canonical link diff, vendor absence probe, rich-text codec + comment limits) have no ticket IDs, while the success criteria asserts 'Every deliverable above is covered by exactly one child' — this claim cannot be traced or enforced without stable child identifiers for those items.
findings.location: Child decomposition — open children list; Success Criteria section
scenarios: If two separate implementation efforts both claim to address 'canonical link diff,' the plan's non-overlap guarantee has no enforcement mechanism — there is no authoritative ticket ID to assert single ownership.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or add) that the `inbound_fields.py` neutrality-gate exemption is expressed as a named constant or inline comment citing ticket `556a-5a1f-adb3-4139` directly inside `test_backend_neutrality.py`, not only in epic prose.
evidence: 'single recorded exemption: `inbound_fields.py`, the Jira InboundMapper implementation — verified by the union of AST/sweep cases in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py`'

Plan does not state the exemption is expressed as a named constant, skip-reason string, or inline `# exemption: tracked by 556a…` comment inside the test file itself — only that it is 'recorded' in the epic.
Follow-up ticket `556a-5a1f-adb3-4139` is linked in plan prose but the durability of that link inside the codebase is unspecified.
findings.finding: The `inbound_fields.py` neutrality-gate exemption is described as 'recorded' but the plan does not specify that this exemption is captured as a named, machine-readable constant or inline comment in `test_backend_neutrality.py` itself — only in the epic plan text and a linked follow-up ticket. If the exemption lives only in prose, a future contributor could remove it during cleanup without realizing it tracks an open architectural debt (ticket `556a-5a1f-adb3-4139`).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Zero vendor reach-through in core' bullet; 'single recorded exemption: `inbound_fields.py`'
scenarios: A future contributor running a sweep-tightening pass sees `inbound_fields.py` in the allowlist, has no in-code rationale, and either removes the exemption (breaking the test) or silently widens the gate.
The follow-up ticket is closed or re-scoped; the exemption in the test file has no breadcrumb back to the architectural decision, making it opaque during review.
findings.suggested_fix: In `test_backend_neutrality.py`, define the exemption as a named set, e.g. `NEUTRALITY_EXEMPTIONS = {'inbound_fields.py'}  # tracked by 556a-5a1f-adb3-4139: pending physical relocation` and reference it in the sweep assertion, so the rationale is self-documenting in the codebase.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign ticket identifiers to the four open children that currently lack them (canonical outbound field diff, canonical link diff, vendor absence probe, rich-text codec + comment limits) so that AC bullets 4 and 5 reference trackable, closeable work items.
evidence: "Canonical-shape outbound field diff" — no ticket ID
"Canonical link diff" — no ticket ID
"Vendor absence probe behind `SupportsAbsenceProbe`" — no ticket ID
"Rich-text codec + comment limits behind the port" — no ticket ID

AC bullet 4: 'verified by the union of AST/sweep cases in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py added by the open children (incl. the removal of that gate's documented reconcile_check carve-out), green on main'
AC bullet 5: 'verified by the canonical-diff children's characterization and neutrality tests, green on main'
findings.finding: Four of the six open children (canonical outbound field diff, canonical link diff, vendor absence probe, rich-text codec + comment limits) carry no ticket identifier, yet the plan's Acceptance Criteria depend on them by name — 'the canonical-diff children's characterization and neutrality tests' and 'the open children (incl. the removal of that gate's documented reconcile_check carve-out)'. Without tracked ticket IDs, the AC's proof chain cannot be verified: there is no binding work item that ensures the neutrality gate becomes carve-out-free or that the canonical-comparison AC goes green on main.
findings.location: Open children list + Acceptance Criteria bullets 4 and 5
scenarios: A reviewer auditing AC compliance cannot confirm the zero-vendor-reach-through or canonical-comparison ACs are met, because the work items that add the neutrality-test cases and remove the reconcile_check carve-out are untracked — those ACs may remain permanently unverifiable if the unidentified children are never assigned.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a ticket ID line (same format as `97f2-2d98-2eff-41c1` and `ad44-9f46-07aa-480c`) to each of the four untracked open-child bullets, and update the AC bullets to reference those IDs explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale sentence to ADR 0035 §(d) (or the `_backend.py` module docstring) explaining why `typing.Protocol` with `@runtime_checkable` was chosen over `abc.ABC` for the Backend interface — specifically that structural subtyping avoids a mandatory import chain from the adapter into `_backend.py`, and that `@runtime_checkable` enables the capability isinstance-guard pattern without requiring explicit capability registration on the backend.
evidence: ADR §(d) documents five role Protocols and three opt-in capability Protocols with their surface areas, but contains no sentence explaining why `typing.Protocol` (structural subtyping) was preferred over `abc.ABC` (nominal subtyping with explicit registration).
_backend.py module docstring says 'pure interface: Protocol declarations plus the RemoteRef identity value' but gives no rationale for why Protocol rather than ABC.

The capability detection mechanism ('Callers detect a capability by an isinstance-guarded check against the backend') only works correctly at runtime because the capability Protocols are @runtime_checkable — this design choice has a specific motivation (avoiding explicit backend registration of capabilities) that is nowhere stated.
findings.finding: The plan states the positive rationale for the canonical-comparison principle and the port-boundary rule thoroughly (Design resolution section, ADR 0035 §(d) corollary), but provides no positive rationale for WHY structural typing via `typing.Protocol` was chosen for the Backend interface definition over alternative mechanisms such as abstract base classes (`abc.ABC`). The ADR §(d) records the interface surface but not the choice of Protocol-based structural subtyping as the enforcement mechanism, leaving an implementer of a second backend without a documented reason why the structural (rather than nominal) typing model was selected.
scenarios: An implementer of the first real second backend (epic be74) reads the ADR and _backend.py to understand the contract, must implement a class satisfying the Protocol structurally but has no documented reason why it should NOT inherit from the Protocol or an ABC — they may introduce ABC inheritance or miss the @runtime_checkable requirement for the capability isinstance checks.

A future reviewer of the Backend port cannot tell from plan or ADR whether Protocol was chosen for duck-typing flexibility, for avoiding import cycles, for standalone-load compatibility with spec_from_file_location, or simply by convention.
findings.suggested_fix: In `_backend.py`'s module docstring, add: 'Protocols (not ABCs) are used so an adapter module need not import from `_backend` at the class-definition level — only at the call site where it constructs a Backend. `@runtime_checkable` on the capability Protocols enables `isinstance(backend, SupportsLinks)` detection without an explicit capability registry; a backend advertises a capability simply by implementing the Protocol's methods.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In child ad44 G1G2 remediation Correction 2, replace 'outbound_field_diff.py:270-279' with the actual file and function name that 625b introduces (e.g. 'outbound_differ.py, function _text_matches or equivalent') and confirm the function name exists in 625b's delivered code.
evidence: ad44 G1G2 remediation Correction 2: 'instead, mirror the canonical differ (outbound_field_diff.py:270-279): fit the local description via the injected OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote(…).get("description") … and compare with rstrip tolerance (the differ's _text_matches)'
child 625b Scope: 'src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py (canonical comparison; neutral helpers; emission-boundary mapping)' — outbound_field_diff.py is not listed
625b Scope has no new-file entry for outbound_field_diff.py; the only test file additions are under 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (new characterization suite; migrated re-export tests)'
findings.finding: Child ad44's G1G2 remediation Correction 2 instructs implementors to 'Mirror the canonical differ (outbound_field_diff.py:270-279)' and calls `_text_matches`, but the predecessor child 625b's scope lists only outbound_differ.py as the target file — no file named outbound_field_diff.py appears anywhere in 625b's produced file-set or scope section. An implementor following ad44's spec would look for outbound_field_diff.py in the tree introduced by 625b and either not find it or find the logic embedded under a different name in outbound_differ.py, making the concrete line reference unverifiable.
findings.location: child ad44 — 'Plan-review remediation', Correction 2; child 625b — Scope section
scenarios: An implementor implementing ad44 (or re-implementing 625b) searches for outbound_field_diff.py and _text_matches, finds neither, and either introduces a new incorrectly-named file or has to reverse-engineer ad44's intent by reading both specs in full.

A reviewer verifying ad44's AC2 ('description comparison uses OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote … verified by a behavior test') cannot confirm the line reference (outbound_field_diff.py:270-279) is valid because 625b's scope says that code lives in outbound_differ.py.
findings.suggested_fix: Update ad44's Correction 2 reference to read 'outbound_differ.py' (the file 625b's scope lists) and replace the hard-coded line number with the function name, e.g. '(the differ's _text_matches helper in outbound_differ.py)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test_backend_contract.py and the FakeBackend (or its test-double equivalent) to eefd's scope, with contract cases for map_remote_links and link_payload_for_relation exercised against both JiraBackend and a FakeBackend that implements SupportsLinks.
evidence: eefd Scope: lists '_backend.py (SupportsLinks: map_remote_links + link_payload_for_relation)', 'adapters/jira/backend.py (implement the two members)', 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (link-diff behavior tests)', 'test_backend_neutrality.py' — test_backend_contract.py and FakeBackend are NOT listed.

ce4d Testing: 'The contract suite is parameterized over a backend_factory fixture and run against both JiraBackend … and an in-memory FakeBackend'; 'a capability Protocol's methods are exercised only when the backend advertises it (observed via behavior)'
ce4d AC2: 'JiraBackend implements Backend; the contract suite passes when parameterized over both JiraBackend (injected mock transport) and FakeBackend'
eefd approach: 'Move both link translations onto the SupportsLinks capability … map_remote_links … link_payload_for_relation'
findings.finding: Child eefd adds two new concrete methods (map_remote_links, link_payload_for_relation) to the SupportsLinks capability Protocol, but its scope omits test_backend_contract.py and the FakeBackend defined in ce4d. If ce4d's contract suite exercises SupportsLinks-capable backends by calling their capability methods, the suite will either fail (JiraBackend's new methods are untested in the contract) or the FakeBackend used by the suite will be missing the new methods — neither is addressed in eefd's scope or testing plan.
findings.location: child eefd — Scope section; child ce4d — Testing / contract suite description
scenarios: After eefd lands, a new contributor runs the ce4d contract suite against the updated JiraBackend and FakeBackend; the suite does not call map_remote_links or link_payload_for_relation, leaving those delegation paths untested by the Protocol-level harness.

A backend author implementing a second backend consults the contract suite to understand what SupportsLinks requires; the suite only reflects the pre-eefd definition and omits the two new required methods, causing silent contract violations.
findings.suggested_fix: In child eefd's Scope and Testing sections, add: 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_contract.py (extend SupportsLinks contract cases for map_remote_links and link_payload_for_relation; update FakeBackend to implement both methods)'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
